title: Prison abolition [Critical Exchange]
author: Terwiel, Davies, Dilts, Konya, Lester, Scott, Tyson

opening_move.description: The exchange opens with an urgent political diagnosis: criminalization, militarized policing, and mass imprisonment are at the heart of the Trump regime — immigration detention is at an all-time high, and prison abolition has become a pressing practical demand.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Prison abolition is not a utopian demand but a politically urgent framework for understanding and dismantling carceral capitalism in the present

theoretical_positioning.description: A 7-author critical exchange developing multiple perspectives on prison abolition as political theory: its relationship to democracy, care, disability, Indigenous decolonisation, and creative prefiguration.

epistemic_mode.qualitative_methods: analysis of carceral statistics, policy analysis, abolitionist movement studies
epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: critical race theory; feminist abolition theory; disability studies; Indigenous political thought
epistemic_mode.notes: Critical exchange (28 pages, 7 authors). Statistical data on incarceration is supportive; the core argument is normative-theoretical for abolition as political vision.

case_type.case_name: Trump immigration detention; US carceral system; abolitionist movements

confidence_notes: Critical exchange (28 pages, 7 authors). Treated as single profile. Qualitative empirical evidence (prison statistics) is supportive of the normative abolitionist argument.
